scenario: A football team is 2-0 down at half-time in a cup final. Historically, comebacks of two goals in finals are rare but memorable.
prompt: What usually happens next most often?
options: The trailing team wins in normal time
The leading team still wins more often than not
The match always goes to penalties
The referee abandons the match
explanation: A two-goal half-time lead is still usually enough — comebacks happen, but the team ahead wins most of the time.

scenario: In a best-of-seven NBA series, a team takes a 3-1 lead.
prompt: What is the most likely outcome from here?
options: The trailing team wins the series more often
The team ahead closes it out more often than not
The series always goes to game 7
Both teams forfeit
explanation: A 3-1 lead is a strong position; the leading side still closes most series.

scenario: A Grand Slam tennis final is on serve at 5-5 in the fifth set.
prompt: What tends to decide the match from here?
options: Whoever holds serve through a tiebreak or breaks first
The player who won the toss always wins
The match is restarted from 0-0
The umpire picks a winner
explanation: Late in a fifth set, a single break of serve or a tiebreak usually separates the champion.

scenario: An F1 race starts under safety car after a wet opening lap.
options: Pit strategy and tyre choices become even more decisive
The race is always cancelled
Overtaking is banned for the rest of the race
Drivers swap cars
explanation: Wet starts and safety cars scramble tyre windows — strategy often decides the result.

scenario: A UFC title fight goes to the judges after five close rounds.
options: The judges' scorecards
Fan vote on social media
Whoever weighed in lighter
A sudden-death sixth round always
explanation: If neither fighter finishes the bout, the judges' scorecards determine the champion.

scenario: A cricket chase needs 12 runs off the final over with 2 wickets in hand.
prompt: What matters most in the next six balls?
options: Dot balls and yorkers vs boundaries and cool heads
The coin toss from the morning
The colour of the kits
Whether the match is a Test or T20 only — nothing else
explanation: Death bowling (yorkers, change-ups) versus clear striking usually decides last-over chases.

scenario: An NHL playoff game is tied after 60 minutes.
prompt: What happens next under modern rules?
options: Sudden-death overtime (and eventually a shootout in some rounds/eras)
The team with more hits wins
Both teams get a point and leave
They replay the entire game next week
explanation: Tied playoff hockey goes to overtime; regular season uses overtime then a shootout.

scenario: A baseball team leads by one run with two outs and a runner on third in the 9th.
prompt: What is the defence trying to force?
options: Any out that ends the inning before the runner scores
An intentional walk to the pitcher only
A forfeit
Extra innings immediately
explanation: With two outs, one more out ends the game — strikeout, groundout, or flyout all work if the run doesn't score first.
